ingredients.item: 1 pound (454 g) cassava roots, peeled, soaked, and grated (see here), or frozen grated cassava, thawed
ingredients.original: 1 pound (454 g) cassava roots, peeled, soaked, and grated (see here), or frozen grated cassava, thawed

ingredients.item: 2 cups (480 ml) vegetable oil, for deep-frying
ingredients.original: 2 cups (480 ml) vegetable oil, for deep-frying

ingredients.item: 1. In a large bowl, combine the grated cassava, salt, sugar, chili powder, scallion, and flour, and mix well.
ingredients.original: 1. In a large bowl, combine the grated cassava, salt, sugar, chili powder, scallion, and flour, and mix well.

ingredients.item: 2. Grab a pinch (about 1 tablespoon) of the paste and roll it between your palms to shape into an oval cocoon that is about 2 inches (5 cm) long and 1 inch (2.5 cm) thick in the middle. Repeat with remaining paste.
ingredients.original: 2. Grab a pinch (about 1 tablespoon) of the paste and roll it between your palms to shape into an oval cocoon that is about 2 inches (5 cm) long and 1 inch (2.5 cm) thick in the middle. Repeat with remaining paste.

ingredients.item: 3. In a small saucepan or a wok, heat the oil over medium heat until it reaches 320°F (160°C) on a cooking thermometer. (You can also dip the pointy end of a wooden chopstick into the oil; if a steady stream of tiny bubbles appears around it, the oil is ready.)
ingredients.original: 3. In a small saucepan or a wok, heat the oil over medium heat until it reaches 320°F (160°C) on a cooking thermometer. (You can also dip the pointy end of a wooden chopstick into the oil; if a steady stream of tiny bubbles appears around it, the oil is ready.)

ingredients.item: 4. Gently drop in the cassava cocoons and fry in batches, turning often, until golden brown (about 15 minutes). With a wire spider or slotted spoon, transfer fritters to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.
ingredients.original: 4. Gently drop in the cassava cocoons and fry in batches, turning often, until golden brown (about 15 minutes). With a wire spider or slotted spoon, transfer fritters to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.

ingredients.item: 5. Serve within 1 hour after frying. You can store any leftovers in an airtight container and re-fry them whenever you are ready to serve.
ingredients.original: 5. Serve within 1 hour after frying. You can store any leftovers in an airtight container and re-fry them whenever you are ready to serve.
